message:
fix(core): scalar function NULL propagation and SUBSTR negative start

Added centralized NULL argument check in eval_scalar_fn: if any argument
is NULL and the function follows standard SQL NULL propagation rules, return
NULL immediately. Exceptions: coalesce, ifnull, nullif, typeof, quote, iif,
max, min (which have explicit NULL handling).

Previously, functions like REPLACE, TRIM, LTRIM, RTRIM, HEX, INSTR, and
SUBSTR called sqlite_value_to_text() on NULL args, converting NULL to empty
string and producing wrong results (e.g., REPLACE(NULL,'a','b') returned ''
instead of NULL).

Also fixed SUBSTR negative start: -1 should count from the last character,
-2 from second-to-last, etc. Previously all negative values were clamped
to 1.

6 regression tests: replace/trim/hex/instr/substr NULL, substr negative.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
